title: National Strategy for Salmonella Screening of Imported Food Products
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Global   food     trade   has   expanded     rapidly  ,   increasing     the     risk     of     transboundary     transmission     of     Salmonella  ,   one     of     the     leading     causes     of     foodborne     illness  .   Imported     food     products  ,   particularly     poultry  ,   eggs  ,   spices  ,   and     ready-to-eat     items  ,   can     carry     pathogenic     Salmonella     strains     that     threaten     public     health     and     disrupt     domestic     markets  .   A     comprehensive     national     strategy     for     systematic     screening     of     imported     foods     can     reduce     the     entry     of     contaminated     products  ,   strengthen     food     safety     governance  ,   and     protect     consumers  .   Integrating     rapid  ,   validated     detection     methods  —  such   as real-  time   PCR   and     culture     confirmation  —  into   border   inspection     protocols     ensures     timely     risk     management  .   Collaborative     engagement     between     regulators  ,   laboratories  ,   and     importers     is     critical     to     harmonize     standards  ,   improve     compliance  ,   and     maintain     trade     efficiency     without     compromising     public     health  .
